Diagnostics and Treatment by Study Period

Retrospective vs Prospective Cohort — CRECOOLT Study

Author

Data Analysis Report

Published

March 20, 2026

Overview

This report compares microbiological diagnostic practices, carbapenem resistance epidemiology, and anti-CRE antibiotic therapy between the retrospective (n = 521) and prospective (n = 171) cohorts of the CRECOOLT study.

NoteScope
  • Screening, resistance mechanisms, and prophylaxis/management strategy: all colonised or infected patients (retrospective n = 244; prospective n = 86)
  • Anti-CRE treatment comparisons: CRE-infected patients only (retrospective n = 136; prospective n = 54)
Code 
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)

# Load data
CRECOOLT_overall <- read_csv("CRECOOLT_overall.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
baseline_data <- CRECOOLT_overall |> filter(is.na(redcap_repeat_instrument))

# ── Cohort definitions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
colonised_data <- baseline_data |>
  filter(cre_colon == 1 | cre_infection == 1) |>
  mutate(period = factor(ifelse(retro_or_pros == 1, "Retrospective", "Prospective"),
                         levels = c("Retrospective", "Prospective")))

infected_data <- baseline_data |>
  filter(cre_infection == 1) |>
  mutate(period = factor(ifelse(retro_or_pros == 1, "Retrospective", "Prospective"),
                         levels = c("Retrospective", "Prospective")))

# ── Antibiotic exposure data ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Select only required columns before joining to avoid name collisions with
# retro_or_pros / cre_infection in the repeated-instrument rows (which are NA)
period_lkp <- baseline_data |>
  select(record_id, .period = retro_or_pros, .cre_inf = cre_infection)

cre_abx <- CRECOOLT_overall |>
  filter(redcap_repeat_instrument == "exposure_to_cre_antibiotics") |>
  select(record_id, redcap_repeat_instance, drug, combination_treat,
         indication, other_drug) |>
  left_join(period_lkp, by = "record_id") |>
  filter(!is.na(.period)) |>
  mutate(period = factor(ifelse(.period == 1, "Retrospective", "Prospective"),
                         levels = c("Retrospective", "Prospective")))

cre_abx_inf <- cre_abx |> filter(.cre_inf == 1)

# ── Constants and helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
drug_labels <- c(
  "1" = "Ertapenem",        "2"  = "Meropenem",
  "3" = "Amikacin",         "4"  = "Gentamicin",
  "5" = "TMP/SMX",          "6"  = "Tigecycline",
  "7" = "Colistin",         "8"  = "Ceftazidime-avibactam",
  "9" = "Meropenem/vaborbactam", "10" = "Imipenem/relebactam",
  "11" = "Cefiderocol",     "12" = "Fosfomycin",
  "13" = "Other"
)

chi_p <- function(tbl) tryCatch(round(chisq.test(tbl)$p.value, 3), error = function(e) NA_real_)
wrs_p <- function(x, g) tryCatch(round(wilcox.test(x ~ g)$p.value, 3), error = function(e) NA_real_)

retro_n_col <- sum(colonised_data$period == "Retrospective")
pros_n_col  <- sum(colonised_data$period == "Prospective")
retro_n_inf <- sum(infected_data$period  == "Retrospective")
pros_n_inf  <- sum(infected_data$period  == "Prospective")

Carbapenem Resistance Mechanisms

Resistance mechanisms were characterised from the CRE colonising strain in all colonised/infected patients.

Code 
mec_labels <- c(
  mec_of_carbapenem_resi___1 = "KPC",
  mec_of_carbapenem_resi___2 = "OXA-48",
  mec_of_carbapenem_resi___3 = "NDM",
  mec_of_carbapenem_resi___4 = "VIM",
  mec_of_carbapenem_resi___5 = "Other"
)

resist_tbl <- map_df(names(mec_labels), function(v) {
  n_r <- sum(colonised_data[colonised_data$period == "Retrospective", v] == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  n_p <- sum(colonised_data[colonised_data$period == "Prospective",   v] == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  data.frame(
    Mechanism             = mec_labels[v],
    `Retrospective (n=244)` = paste0(n_r, " (", round(n_r / retro_n_col * 100, 1), "%)"),
    `Prospective (n=86)`    = paste0(n_p, " (", round(n_p / pros_n_col  * 100, 1), "%)"),
    p_value = chi_p(matrix(c(n_r, retro_n_col - n_r, n_p, pros_n_col - n_p), 2)),
    check.names = FALSE
  )
})

knitr::kable(resist_tbl,
  caption = "Carbapenem resistance mechanisms in colonised/infected patients by study period")
Carbapenem resistance mechanisms in colonised/infected patients by study period
Mechanism Retrospective (n=244) Prospective (n=86) p_value
mec_of_carbapenem_resi___1 KPC 170 (69.7%) 54 (62.8%) 0.298
mec_of_carbapenem_resi___2 OXA-48 16 (6.6%) 6 (7%) 1.000
mec_of_carbapenem_resi___3 NDM 7 (2.9%) 10 (11.6%) 0.004
mec_of_carbapenem_resi___4 VIM 6 (2.5%) 3 (3.5%) 0.905
mec_of_carbapenem_resi___5 Other 24 (9.8%) 6 (7%) 0.565
Code 
resist_plot_data <- map_df(names(mec_labels), function(v) {
  n_r <- sum(colonised_data[colonised_data$period == "Retrospective", v] == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  n_p <- sum(colonised_data[colonised_data$period == "Prospective",   v] == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  data.frame(
    Mechanism = mec_labels[v],
    Retrospective = n_r / retro_n_col * 100,
    Prospective   = n_p / pros_n_col  * 100
  )
}) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Retrospective, Prospective),
               names_to = "Period", values_to = "Percent") |>
  mutate(Period = factor(Period, levels = c("Retrospective", "Prospective")))

ggplot(resist_plot_data, aes(x = Mechanism, y = Percent, fill = Period)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Percent, 1), "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Retrospective" = "#1565C0", "Prospective" = "#E53935"),
                    name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 85), breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +
  labs(title = "Carbapenem Resistance Mechanisms by Study Period",
       subtitle = "Colonised/infected patients (retrospective n=244; prospective n=86)",
       x = "Resistance Mechanism", y = "Prevalence (%)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
        legend.position = "top")

Key finding: NDM increased significantly in the prospective cohort (2.9% → 11.6%, p = 0.004), suggesting a change in the predominant resistance epidemiology. KPC remained the most common mechanism in both periods.

Microbiological Screening Intensity

Numbers of microbiological samples collected per patient during the follow-up period.

Code 
screen_vars   <- c(blood = "micro_screen_blood",   resp  = "micro_screen_resp",
                   urine = "micro_screen_urino",    abd   = "micro_screen_intra_abd")
screen_labels <- c(blood = "Blood cultures",        resp  = "Respiratory samples",
                   urine = "Urine cultures",         abd   = "Intra-abdominal samples")

screen_tbl <- map_df(names(screen_vars), function(k) {
  x <- colonised_data[[screen_vars[k]]]
  g <- colonised_data$period
  r <- x[g == "Retrospective"]; p <- x[g == "Prospective"]
  data.frame(
    `Sample type` = screen_labels[k],
    `Retrospective [median (IQR)]` = paste0(
      median(r, na.rm=T), " (", quantile(r, .25, na.rm=T), "–", quantile(r, .75, na.rm=T), ")"),
    `Prospective [median (IQR)]` = paste0(
      median(p, na.rm=T), " (", quantile(p, .25, na.rm=T), "–", quantile(p, .75, na.rm=T), ")"),
    p_value = wrs_p(x, g),
    check.names = FALSE
  )
})

knitr::kable(screen_tbl,
  caption = "Microbiological screening intensity by study period (colonised/infected patients)")
Microbiological screening intensity by study period (colonised/infected patients)
Sample type Retrospective [median (IQR)] Prospective [median (IQR)] p_value
blood Blood cultures 5 (2–11) 9 (4–16) 0.002
resp Respiratory samples 0 (0–1) 0 (0–1) 0.373
urine Urine cultures 2 (1–4) 2.5 (1–4) 0.090
abd Intra-abdominal samples 2 (1–4) 3 (1.75–6) 0.007
Code 
screen_plot <- map_df(names(screen_vars), function(k) {
  df <- colonised_data[, c("period", screen_vars[k])]
  names(df)[2] <- "n_samples"
  df$sample <- screen_labels[k]
  df
}) |> filter(!is.na(n_samples))

ggplot(screen_plot, aes(x = period, y = n_samples, fill = period)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = 21, outlier.size = 1.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~sample, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Retrospective" = "#1565C0", "Prospective" = "#E53935"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "Microbiological Screening Intensity by Study Period",
       subtitle = "Colonised/infected patients — number of samples per patient",
       x = NULL, y = "Number of samples") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
        legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

Key finding: Blood culture and intra-abdominal sampling intensity increased significantly in the prospective period (blood: median 5 vs 9, p = 0.002; intra-abdominal: 2 vs 3, p = 0.007), likely reflecting more structured surveillance protocols in the prospective phase.

Colonisation Management Strategy

Code 
strat_labels <- c(
  cre_colonization___1 = "No strategy adopted",
  cre_colonization___2 = "Targeted peri-operative prophylaxis",
  cre_colonization___3 = "Pre-emptive strategy"
)

strat_tbl <- map_df(names(strat_labels), function(v) {
  n_r <- sum(colonised_data[colonised_data$period == "Retrospective", v] == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  n_p <- sum(colonised_data[colonised_data$period == "Prospective",   v] == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  data.frame(
    Strategy              = strat_labels[v],
    `Retrospective (n=244)` = paste0(n_r, " (", round(n_r / retro_n_col * 100, 1), "%)"),
    `Prospective (n=86)`    = paste0(n_p, " (", round(n_p / pros_n_col  * 100, 1), "%)"),
    p_value = chi_p(matrix(c(n_r, retro_n_col - n_r, n_p, pros_n_col - n_p), 2)),
    check.names = FALSE
  )
})

knitr::kable(strat_tbl,
  caption = "Colonisation management strategy by study period (colonised/infected patients)")
Colonisation management strategy by study period (colonised/infected patients)
Strategy Retrospective (n=244) Prospective (n=86) p_value
cre_colonization___1 No strategy adopted 134 (54.9%) 57 (66.3%) 0.088
cre_colonization___2 Targeted peri-operative prophylaxis 74 (30.3%) 20 (23.3%) 0.267
cre_colonization___3 Pre-emptive strategy 37 (15.2%) 11 (12.8%) 0.720
Code 
strat_plot_data <- map_df(names(strat_labels), function(v) {
  n_r <- sum(colonised_data[colonised_data$period == "Retrospective", v] == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  n_p <- sum(colonised_data[colonised_data$period == "Prospective",   v] == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  data.frame(
    Strategy = strat_labels[v],
    Retrospective = n_r / retro_n_col * 100,
    Prospective   = n_p / pros_n_col  * 100
  )
}) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Retrospective, Prospective),
               names_to = "Period", values_to = "Percent") |>
  mutate(Period = factor(Period, levels = c("Retrospective", "Prospective")))

ggplot(strat_plot_data, aes(x = reorder(Strategy, -Percent), y = Percent, fill = Period)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Percent, 1), "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Retrospective" = "#1565C0", "Prospective" = "#E53935"),
                    name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 75), breaks = seq(0, 70, 10)) +
  labs(title = "Colonisation Management Strategy by Study Period",
       subtitle = "Colonised/infected patients",
       x = NULL, y = "Percentage of patients (%)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
        legend.position = "top",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

Anti-CRE Treatment in Infected Patients

Agents Used

Exposure-level data: a single patient may have received more than one agent.

Code 
drug_tbl <- cre_abx_inf |>
  filter(!is.na(drug)) |>
  mutate(drug_name = drug_labels[as.character(drug)]) |>
  count(period, drug_name) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = period, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) |>
  mutate(
    p_value = map2_dbl(Retrospective, Prospective,
      ~ chi_p(matrix(c(.x, retro_n_inf - .x, .y, pros_n_inf - .y), 2))),
    Retrospective = paste0(Retrospective, " (", round(Retrospective / retro_n_inf * 100, 1), "%)"),
    Prospective   = paste0(Prospective,   " (", round(Prospective   / pros_n_inf  * 100, 1), "%)")
  ) |>
  arrange(drug_name) |>
  rename(Drug = drug_name,
         `Retrospective (n=136)` = Retrospective,
         `Prospective (n=54)`    = Prospective)

knitr::kable(drug_tbl,
  caption = "Anti-CRE agents used in infected patients by study period (patients may receive >1 agent)")
Anti-CRE agents used in infected patients by study period (patients may receive >1 agent)
Drug Retrospective (n=136) Prospective (n=54) p_value
Amikacin 34 (25%) 7 (13%) 0.104
Cefiderocol 6 (4.4%) 1 (1.9%) 0.676
Ceftazidime-avibactam 76 (55.9%) 42 (77.8%) 0.008
Colistin 47 (34.6%) 6 (11.1%) 0.002
Ertapenem 5 (3.7%) 0 (0%) 0.355
Fosfomycin 17 (12.5%) 1 (1.9%) 0.047
Gentamicin 8 (5.9%) 0 (0%) 0.155
Meropenem 92 (67.6%) 34 (63%) 0.656
Meropenem/vaborbactam 4 (2.9%) 5 (9.3%) 0.141
Other 17 (12.5%) 14 (25.9%) 0.041
TMP/SMX 3 (2.2%) 0 (0%) 0.649
Tigecycline 51 (37.5%) 18 (33.3%) 0.710
Code 
drug_plot_data <- cre_abx_inf |>
  filter(!is.na(drug)) |>
  mutate(drug_name = drug_labels[as.character(drug)]) |>
  count(period, drug_name) |>
  mutate(pct = ifelse(period == "Retrospective",
                      n / retro_n_inf * 100,
                      n / pros_n_inf  * 100)) |>
  filter(drug_name != "Other")

# Highlight significantly changed drugs
sig_drugs <- c("Ceftazidime-avibactam", "Colistin", "Fosfomycin")

ggplot(drug_plot_data,
       aes(x = reorder(drug_name, pct), y = pct, fill = period)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(pct, 0), "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), hjust = -0.15, size = 3.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Retrospective" = "#1565C0", "Prospective" = "#E53935"),
                    name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 95), breaks = seq(0, 90, 20)) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Anti-CRE Drug Use by Study Period",
       subtitle = "CRE-infected patients (retrospective n=136; prospective n=54)",
       x = NULL, y = "% of infected patients receiving agent") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
        legend.position = "top")

Key findings:

  • Ceftazidime-avibactam use increased markedly (55.9% → 77.8%, p = 0.008), reflecting its emergence as the preferred backbone agent for KPC-producers
  • Colistin use declined substantially (34.6% → 11.1%, p = 0.002), consistent with the shift away from polymyxin-based regimens given their toxicity profile
  • Fosfomycin use also declined (12.5% → 1.9%, p = 0.047), likely replaced by newer β-lactam/β-lactamase inhibitor combinations
  • Meropenem remained in common use in both periods (~68% vs 63%), typically as part of combination regimens or for susceptible isolates

Combination Therapy and Treatment Indication

Code 
# Combination therapy — patient level (first record per patient)
combo_pts <- cre_abx_inf |>
  filter(!is.na(combination_treat)) |>
  distinct(record_id, .keep_all = TRUE)

n_cr <- sum(combo_pts$period == "Retrospective" & combo_pts$combination_treat == 1)
n_cp <- sum(combo_pts$period == "Prospective"   & combo_pts$combination_treat == 1)
nr_c <- sum(combo_pts$period == "Retrospective")
np_c <- sum(combo_pts$period == "Prospective")

combo_row <- data.frame(
  Metric                  = "Combination therapy",
  `Retrospective (n=136)` = paste0(n_cr, " (", round(n_cr / nr_c * 100, 1), "%)"),
  `Prospective (n=54)`    = paste0(n_cp, " (", round(n_cp / np_c * 100, 1), "%)"),
  p_value = chi_p(matrix(c(n_cr, nr_c - n_cr, n_cp, np_c - n_cp), 2)),
  check.names = FALSE
)

# Indication
ind_wide <- cre_abx_inf |>
  filter(!is.na(indication)) |>
  mutate(ind_label = case_when(
    indication == 1 ~ "Empiric (symptoms onset)",
    indication == 2 ~ "CRECOOLT score-guided",
    indication == 3 ~ "Targeted (documented infection)"
  )) |>
  count(period, ind_label) |>
  group_by(period) |>
  mutate(pct   = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1),
         label = paste0(n, " (", pct, "%)")) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(period, ind_label, label) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = period, values_from = label, values_fill = "0 (0%)") |>
  rename(Metric                  = ind_label,
         `Retrospective (n=136)` = Retrospective,
         `Prospective (n=54)`    = Prospective) |>
  mutate(p_value = NA_real_)

bind_rows(combo_row, ind_wide) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Combination therapy and treatment indication by study period")
Combination therapy and treatment indication by study period
Metric Retrospective (n=136) Prospective (n=54) p_value
Combination therapy 98 (73.1%) 20 (39.2%) 0
Empiric (symptoms onset) 147 (41.5%) 42 (32.8%) NA
Targeted (documented infection) 207 (58.5%) 79 (61.7%) NA
CRECOOLT score-guided 0 (0%) 7 (5.5%) NA
Code 
# Combination therapy bar
combo_df <- data.frame(
  Period    = c("Retrospective", "Prospective"),
  Combo_pct = c(round(n_cr / nr_c * 100, 1), round(n_cp / np_c * 100, 1))
) |> mutate(Period = factor(Period, levels = c("Retrospective", "Prospective")))

p_combo <- ggplot(combo_df, aes(x = Period, y = Combo_pct, fill = Period)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(Combo_pct, "%")), vjust = -0.5, size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Retrospective" = "#1565C0", "Prospective" = "#E53935")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 90), breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +
  labs(title = "Combination Therapy", x = NULL, y = "% patients") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none", plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

# Indication stacked bar
ind_plot_data <- cre_abx_inf |>
  filter(!is.na(indication)) |>
  mutate(ind_label = case_when(
    indication == 1 ~ "Empiric",
    indication == 2 ~ "Score-guided",
    indication == 3 ~ "Targeted"
  )) |>
  count(period, ind_label) |>
  group_by(period) |>
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n) * 100)

p_ind <- ggplot(ind_plot_data, aes(x = period, y = pct, fill = ind_label)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(pct, 0), "%")),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), color = "white",
            fontface = "bold", size = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Empiric" = "#EF6C00", "Score-guided" = "#2E7D32", "Targeted" = "#4527A0"),
    name = "Indication") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 25)) +
  labs(title = "Treatment Indication", x = NULL, y = "% antibiotic exposures") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "right")

p_combo | p_ind +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Treatment Patterns by Study Period",
    subtitle = "CRE-infected patients"
  )

Key findings:

  • Combination therapy declined sharply between periods (73.1% → 39.2%, p < 0.001), consistent with the transition to novel β-lactam/β-lactamase inhibitor agents that are effective as monotherapy
  • CRECOOLT score-guided therapy appeared for the first time in the prospective cohort (5.5%), reflecting real-world adoption of the prediction model in clinical decision-making

Summary

Code 
summary_df <- data.frame(
  Finding = c(
    "NDM resistance mechanism",
    "Blood culture intensity (median)",
    "Intra-abdominal sampling (median)",
    "Ceftazidime-avibactam use",
    "Colistin use",
    "Combination therapy"
  ),
  Retrospective = c("2.9%", "5 (IQR 2–11)", "2 (IQR 1–4)", "55.9%", "34.6%", "73.1%"),
  Prospective   = c("11.6%", "9 (IQR 4–16)", "3 (IQR 1.75–6)", "77.8%", "11.1%", "39.2%"),
  p_value       = c("0.004", "0.002", "0.007", "0.008", "0.002", "<0.001"),
  Direction     = c("↑", "↑", "↑", "↑", "↓", "↓")
)

knitr::kable(summary_df,
  caption = "Summary of statistically significant changes between study periods")
Summary of statistically significant changes between study periods
Finding Retrospective Prospective p_value Direction
NDM resistance mechanism 2.9% 11.6% 0.004 ↑
Blood culture intensity (median) 5 (IQR 2–11) 9 (IQR 4–16) 0.002 ↑
Intra-abdominal sampling (median) 2 (IQR 1–4) 3 (IQR 1.75–6) 0.007 ↑
Ceftazidime-avibactam use 55.9% 77.8% 0.008 ↑
Colistin use 34.6% 11.1% 0.002 ↓
Combination therapy 73.1% 39.2% <0.001 ↓

Survival Analysis by CRE Therapy and Resistance Mechanism

Kaplan-Meier survival curves from time of CRE infection to death, censored at hospital discharge or 90 days. Patients are classified by:

  • Therapy group: any exposure to ceftazidime-avibactam, meropenem-vaborbactam, or cefiderocol (“Novel BL/BLI”) vs other agents
  • Carbapenemase mechanism: KPC vs Non-KPC (OXA-48, NDM, VIM, or other)
Code 
library(survival)
library(survminer)

# ── JAMA-style theme ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
jama_theme <- theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12, hjust = 0),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0, color = "grey40"),
    axis.title         = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text          = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.title       = element_blank(),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position    = "top",
    legend.key.width   = unit(1.2, "cm"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "grey90", linewidth = 0.3),
    strip.background   = element_blank(),
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

# ── Therapy classification per patient ───────────────────────────────────────
# Novel BL/BLI = any exposure to ceftazidime-avibactam (8),
#                meropenem-vaborbactam (9), or cefiderocol (11)
therapy_per_patient <- cre_abx_inf |>
  group_by(record_id) |>
  summarise(
    new_therapy  = any(drug %in% c(8, 9, 11), na.rm = TRUE),
    combination  = any(combination_treat == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ── Survival dataset ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
surv_infected <- infected_data |>
  left_join(therapy_per_patient, by = "record_id") |>
  mutate(
    surv_time = case_when(
      !is.na(death_date) & !is.na(cre_infection_date) ~
        as.numeric(death_date - cre_infection_date),
      !is.na(discharge_date) & !is.na(cre_infection_date) ~
        as.numeric(discharge_date - cre_infection_date),
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    surv_event = ifelse(death == 1, 1, 0),
    surv_time  = ifelse(surv_time < 0, NA_real_, surv_time),

    therapy_group = factor(
      ifelse(new_therapy, "Novel BL/BLI", "Other therapies"),
      levels = c("Novel BL/BLI", "Other therapies")
    ),
    combo_group = factor(
      case_when(
        new_therapy    ~ "Novel BL/BLI",
        combination    ~ "Combination (other agents)",
        TRUE           ~ "Monotherapy (other agents)"
      ),
      levels = c("Novel BL/BLI", "Combination (other agents)",
                 "Monotherapy (other agents)")
    ),
    carbapenemase = case_when(
      cre_isolate___1 == 1 ~ "KPC",
      cre_isolate___2 == 1 ~ "OXA-48",
      cre_isolate___3 == 1 ~ "VIM",
      cre_isolate___4 == 1 ~ "NDM",
      cre_isolate___5 == 1 ~ "Other/Unknown",
      TRUE                 ~ "Not typed"
    ),
    carba_group = factor(
      ifelse(carbapenemase == "KPC",
             "KPC",
             "Non-KPC (OXA-48/NDM/VIM/Other)"),
      levels = c("KPC", "Non-KPC (OXA-48/NDM/VIM/Other)")
    )
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(surv_time), surv_time >= 0, !is.na(surv_event))

Novel BL/BLI vs Other Therapies

Code 
fit1 <- survfit(Surv(surv_time, surv_event) ~ therapy_group,
                data = surv_infected)

ggsurvplot(
  fit1,
  data              = surv_infected,
  pval              = TRUE,
  pval.size         = 3.5,
  conf.int          = TRUE,
  conf.int.alpha    = 0.12,
  risk.table        = TRUE,
  risk.table.height = 0.22,
  risk.table.title  = "No. at risk",
  risk.table.fontsize = 3,
  xlab              = "Days from CRE Infection",
  ylab              = "Overall Survival",
  title             = "A  Novel BL/BLI vs Other Therapies",
  subtitle          = "CRE-infected patients (n=165); censored at discharge or 90 days",
  legend.labs       = c("Novel BL/BLI (CAZ-AVI / MER-VAB / CEF)",
                        "Other therapies"),
  palette           = c("#374E55", "#DF8F44"),
  xlim              = c(0, 90),
  break.x.by        = 15,
  ylim              = c(0, 1),
  surv.scale        = "percent",
  ggtheme           = jama_theme,
  tables.theme      = theme_cleantable()
)

Novel BL/BLI vs Combination vs Monotherapy

Code 
fit2 <- survfit(Surv(surv_time, surv_event) ~ combo_group,
                data = surv_infected)

ggsurvplot(
  fit2,
  data              = surv_infected,
  pval              = TRUE,
  pval.size         = 3.5,
  conf.int          = TRUE,
  conf.int.alpha    = 0.12,
  risk.table        = TRUE,
  risk.table.height = 0.25,
  risk.table.title  = "No. at risk",
  risk.table.fontsize = 3,
  xlab              = "Days from CRE Infection",
  ylab              = "Overall Survival",
  title             = "B  Novel BL/BLI vs Combination vs Monotherapy",
  subtitle          = "Combination and monotherapy groups exclude novel BL/BLI agents",
  legend.labs       = c("Novel BL/BLI",
                        "Combination (other agents)",
                        "Monotherapy (other agents)"),
  palette           = c("#374E55", "#DF8F44", "#B24745"),
  xlim              = c(0, 90),
  break.x.by        = 15,
  ylim              = c(0, 1),
  surv.scale        = "percent",
  ggtheme           = jama_theme,
  tables.theme      = theme_cleantable()
)

Survival by Carbapenemase Mechanism

Code 
surv_typed <- surv_infected |> filter(carbapenemase != "Not typed")
fit3 <- survfit(Surv(surv_time, surv_event) ~ carba_group,
                data = surv_typed)

ggsurvplot(
  fit3,
  data              = surv_typed,
  pval              = TRUE,
  pval.size         = 3.5,
  conf.int          = TRUE,
  conf.int.alpha    = 0.12,
  risk.table        = TRUE,
  risk.table.height = 0.22,
  risk.table.title  = "No. at risk",
  risk.table.fontsize = 3,
  xlab              = "Days from CRE Infection",
  ylab              = "Overall Survival",
  title             = "C  Survival by Carbapenemase Mechanism",
  subtitle          = "Non-KPC includes OXA-48, NDM, VIM, and other mechanisms",
  legend.labs       = c("KPC", "Non-KPC (OXA-48/NDM/VIM/Other)"),
  palette           = c("#374E55", "#DF8F44"),
  xlim              = c(0, 90),
  break.x.by        = 15,
  ylim              = c(0, 1),
  surv.scale        = "percent",
  ggtheme           = jama_theme,
  tables.theme      = theme_cleantable()
)

Therapy Effect Stratified by Carbapenemase Mechanism

Code 
surv4 <- surv_infected |>
  filter(carbapenemase != "Not typed") |>
  mutate(
    strata_label = factor(
      paste0(therapy_group, "\n(", carba_group, ")"),
      levels = c(
        "Novel BL/BLI\n(KPC)",
        "Novel BL/BLI\n(Non-KPC (OXA-48/NDM/VIM/Other))",
        "Other therapies\n(KPC)",
        "Other therapies\n(Non-KPC (OXA-48/NDM/VIM/Other))"
      )
    )
  )

fit4 <- survfit(Surv(surv_time, surv_event) ~ strata_label, data = surv4)

ggsurvplot(
  fit4,
  data              = surv4,
  pval              = TRUE,
  pval.size         = 3.5,
  conf.int          = TRUE,
  conf.int.alpha    = 0.10,
  risk.table        = TRUE,
  risk.table.height = 0.28,
  risk.table.title  = "No. at risk",
  risk.table.fontsize = 2.8,
  xlab              = "Days from CRE Infection",
  ylab              = "Overall Survival",
  title             = "D  Therapy Effect Stratified by Carbapenemase Mechanism",
  subtitle          = "Novel BL/BLI vs other therapies within KPC and Non-KPC groups",
  legend.labs       = c("Novel BL/BLI — KPC",
                        "Novel BL/BLI — Non-KPC",
                        "Other therapies — KPC",
                        "Other therapies — Non-KPC"),
  palette           = c("#374E55", "#80A0A8", "#DF8F44", "#F2C27A"),
  xlim              = c(0, 90),
  break.x.by        = 15,
  ylim              = c(0, 1),
  surv.scale        = "percent",
  ggtheme           = jama_theme,
  tables.theme      = theme_cleantable()
)
NoteInterpretation Notes
  • Novel BL/BLI = ceftazidime-avibactam, meropenem-vaborbactam, or cefiderocol (any exposure during infection episode). Patients may also have received other agents concurrently.
  • Time origin = date of CRE infection; censored at hospital discharge or 90 days.
  • Carbapenemase groups: KPC (n=125); Non-KPC (n=40); not typed (n=3, excluded from plots C and D).
  • These are observational, unadjusted survival curves. Differences may reflect confounding by infection severity, source, or era.

Adjusted Cox Proportional Hazards Model

To account for potential confounding by study period, disease severity, age, and infection source, a multivariable Cox model was fitted with therapy group (Novel BL/BLI vs other) as the primary exposure.

Code 
# ── Build model dataset ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cox_data <- surv_infected |>
  mutate(
    infect_src = factor(
      case_when(
        infection_source___1 == 1                              ~ "Bloodstream",
        infection_source___2 == 1                              ~ "Respiratory",
        infection_source___3 == 1                              ~ "Urinary",
        infection_source___4 == 1 | infection_source___5 == 1 ~ "Surgical/Abd",
        infection_source___6 == 1                              ~ "Other",
        TRUE                                                   ~ NA_character_
      ),
      levels = c("Bloodstream", "Respiratory", "Urinary", "Surgical/Abd", "Other")
    ),
    therapy_fac = relevel(factor(therapy_group), ref = "Other therapies"),
    period_fac  = relevel(factor(period),         ref = "Retrospective"),
    meld_s = as.numeric(scale(meld_score)),   # standardised (per SD)
    age_s  = as.numeric(scale(age))
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(meld_score), !is.na(infect_src))

# ── Fit model ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cox_fit <- coxph(
  Surv(surv_time, surv_event) ~
    therapy_fac + period_fac + meld_s + age_s + infect_src,
  data = cox_data
)

# ── Extract HR table ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cox_tbl <- broom::tidy(cox_fit, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    label = recode(term,
      "therapy_facNovel BL/BLI"    = "Novel BL/BLI vs Other",
      "period_facProspective"      = "Prospective vs Retrospective",
      "meld_s"                     = "MELD score (per SD)",
      "age_s"                      = "Age (per SD)",
      "infect_srcRespiratory"      = "Respiratory vs Bloodstream",
      "infect_srcUrinary"          = "Urinary vs Bloodstream",
      "infect_srcSurgical/Abd"     = "Surgical/Abd vs Bloodstream",
      "infect_srcOther"            = "Other source vs Bloodstream"
    ),
    sig = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, "*", "")
  ) |>
  select(label, HR = estimate, `95% CI lower` = conf.low,
         `95% CI upper` = conf.high, `p value` = p.value)

knitr::kable(cox_tbl, digits = c(0, 2, 2, 2, 3),
  caption = paste0("Adjusted Cox proportional hazards model: all-cause mortality ",
                   "from CRE infection (n = ", nrow(cox_data),
                   ", events = ", sum(cox_data$surv_event), ")"))
Adjusted Cox proportional hazards model: all-cause mortality from CRE infection (n = 160, events = 66)
label HR 95% CI lower 95% CI upper p value
Novel BL/BLI vs Other 0.38 0.21 0.70 0.002
Prospective vs Retrospective 2.14 1.19 3.84 0.011
MELD score (per SD) 1.32 0.99 1.75 0.055
Age (per SD) 1.16 0.90 1.51 0.257
Respiratory vs Bloodstream 0.95 0.40 2.22 0.900
Urinary vs Bloodstream 1.85 0.82 4.17 0.139
Surgical/Abd vs Bloodstream 1.60 0.81 3.13 0.173
Other source vs Bloodstream 1.11 0.31 4.01 0.875

Forest Plot

Code 
forest_data <- cox_tbl |>
  rename(HR = HR, lower = `95% CI lower`, upper = `95% CI upper`, p = `p value`) |>
  mutate(
    label = factor(label, levels = rev(label)),
    group = case_when(
      grepl("Novel|Prospective", label) ~ "Primary",
      grepl("MELD|Age", label)          ~ "Continuous",
      TRUE                               ~ "Infection source"
    ),
    sig     = p < 0.05,
    hr_text = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f\u2013%.2f)", HR, lower, upper),
    p_text  = ifelse(p < 0.001, "<0.001",
               ifelse(p < 0.01,  sprintf("%.3f", p),
                                  sprintf("%.2f",  p)))
  )

ggplot(forest_data, aes(x = HR, y = label)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50",
             linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper, colour = group),
                orientation = "y", width = 0.25, linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = group, shape = sig), size = 3.5) +
  # HR and p-value columns (positioned within scale range)
  geom_text(aes(x = 20, label = hr_text),
            hjust = 1, size = 3.0, family = "mono") +
  geom_text(aes(x = 28, label = p_text),
            hjust = 1, size = 3.0, family = "mono") +
  # Column headers
  annotate("text", x = 20, y = 9.35, label = "HR (95% CI)",
           hjust = 1, size = 3.2, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 28, y = 9.35, label = "p",
           hjust = 1, size = 3.2, fontface = "bold") +
  # Group dividers
  annotate("segment", x = 0.12, xend = 5.5, y = 5.5, yend = 5.5,
           colour = "grey80", linewidth = 0.3) +
  annotate("segment", x = 0.12, xend = 5.5, y = 3.5, yend = 3.5,
           colour = "grey80", linewidth = 0.3) +
  # Vertical divider before text columns
  annotate("segment", x = 6.5, xend = 6.5, y = 0.5, yend = 9,
           colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3) +
  # Group labels
  annotate("text", x = 0.13, y = 8.75, label = "Treatment & Period",
           hjust = 0, size = 2.9, fontface = "bold.italic", colour = "grey35") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.13, y = 5.2,  label = "Patient characteristics",
           hjust = 0, size = 2.9, fontface = "bold.italic", colour = "grey35") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.13, y = 3.2,
           label = "Infection source (ref: Bloodstream)",
           hjust = 0, size = 2.9, fontface = "bold.italic", colour = "grey35") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    trans  = "log",
    limits = c(0.12, 30),
    breaks = c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4),
    labels = c("0.25", "0.5", "1", "2", "4"),
    name   = "Hazard Ratio (log scale)"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("Primary"          = "#374E55",
               "Continuous"       = "#DF8F44",
               "Infection source" = "#B24745"),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("FALSE" = 16, "TRUE" = 18),
    guide  = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Adjusted Cox Proportional Hazards Model",
    subtitle = paste0("Outcome: all-cause mortality from CRE infection",
                      "  |  n = ", nrow(cox_data),
                      ", events = ", sum(cox_data$surv_event)),
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 9.5, colour = "grey40"),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 9.5),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = 9.5),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(colour = "grey92", linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.line.y        = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y       = element_blank()
  )
WarningResidual Confounding

The prospective cohort period and novel BL/BLI use are correlated by design (novel agents became available over time). Even after adjustment, the HR for novel BL/BLI (HR 0.38, 95% CI 0.21–0.70) and the HR for prospective period both remain significant. This collinearity should be interpreted with caution; the two effects may not be fully separable in this dataset.

Technical Notes

Data Source: CRECOOLT study database (N=692 liver transplant patients)

Statistical Methods:

  • Chi-square test (or Fisher’s exact test where cell counts < 5) for categorical variables
  • Wilcoxon rank-sum test for continuous screening counts
  • Antibiotic use proportions are exposure-level (patients may appear multiple times)
  • Combination therapy proportions are patient-level (first record per patient)

Software: R version 4.5.1, with tidyverse and patchwork packages

Report Generated: March 20, 2026
